publications.ref: Chick JM, Kolippakkam D, Nusinow DP, Zhai B, Rad R, Huttlin EL, Gygi SP. A mass-tolerant database search identifies a large proportion of unassigned spectra in shotgun proteomics as modified peptides. Nat Biotechnol. 2015 Jun 15

Characteristics[CellLine].evidence: In a proteome-wide dataset on HEK293 cells (9,513 proteins and 396,736 peptides)

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) spectra acquired in shotgun proteomics experiments

Characteristics[Label].reasoning: Ultra-tolerant database search for modified peptides suggests label-free approach, as labeling methods would be mentioned if used
Characteristics[Label].needs: Explicit mention of labeling strategy or confirmation from methods section

Characteristics[Treatment].reasoning: Study specifically examines peptides with modifications including phosphorylation, glycosylation, methylation
Characteristics[Treatment].needs: Information about whether modifications were induced by specific treatments or are endogenous

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: Confirmed HEK293 human cell line used in shotgun proteomics experiment analyzing whole cell proteome. Study focuses on identifying modified peptides using ultra-tolerant database search. Labeling strategy unclear but likely label-free. Treatment conditions and specific experimental setup need clarification from methods section.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: digested overnight at room temperature with Lys-C (1:200 enzyme to protein ratio) followed by dilution to 1.5M urea and 4 hour digestion with trypsin (1:200 enzyme to protein ratio)

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: alkylation with 10 mM iodoacetamide for 30 minutes at room temperature in the dark

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: The lysate was reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol for 30 minutes at 56ºC

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No labeling reagents mentioned in sample preparation protocol, only chemical treatments for reduction, alkylation and digestion

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: Basic pH reverse phase fractionation that was performed on ~0.5 mg sample... 50 min linear gradient from 5% to 35% acetonitrile in 10mM ammonium bicarbonate pH 8

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: separated the peptide mixture into a total of 96 fractions which were consolidated into 24

Comment[Separation].evidence: Peptides were separated using a 3 hr gradient of 6 to 30% acetonitrile gradient in 0.125% formic acid

Characteristics[Modification].reasoning: Static cysteine alkylation confirmed (+57.02146 Da = carbamidomethyl). Dynamic modifications mentioned in closed search include oxidation (M), deamidation (N,Q), phosphorylation (ST), acetylation (K), methylation (K)
Characteristics[Modification].needs: Clarification on which modifications are relevant for SDRF reporting vs search parameters

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation protocol confirmed: DTT reduction, iodoacetamide alkylation, sequential Lys-C/trypsin digestion. Label-free approach confirmed. HEK293 samples underwent 24-fraction basic pH reverse phase fractionation. Multiple modifications detected including carbamidomethyl (static), oxidation, phosphorylation, acetylation, methylation. Need to determine which modifications should be explicitly listed in SDRF format.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: Basic pH reverse phase fractions from HEK293 cells were analyzed on a Q-Exactive Orbitrap mass spectrometer

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: the top 20 most abundant ions were selected for isolation and fragmentation by HCD

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: From previous step confirmation and DDA method described

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: Liquid chromatography electrospray ionization tandem mass spectrometry (LC-ESI-MS/MS)

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: Q-Exactive Orbitrap mass spectrometer with HCD performed using resolution of 1.75 × 10^4

Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: Normalized collision energies were set to 25% (for Q-Exactive analysis of HEK293 samples)

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: Closed searches were performed with Sequest using indexed databases with a precursor ion tolerance of 5 ppm

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: The fragment ion tolerance was set very narrow (0.01 Da)

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: Specificity was set based on the protease used (trypsin for HEK293 studies) and allowing 1 missed cleavage

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: Peptides were separated using a 3 hr gradient of 6 to 30% acetonitrile gradient

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: with a flow rate of ∼300 nL/min

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Q Exactive Orbitrap mass spectrometer parameters fully confirmed: HCD fragmentation at 25% collision energy, nanoESI ionization, 3-hour LC gradients at 300 nL/min flow rate. Search parameters established with 5 ppm precursor and 0.01 Da fragment tolerances, allowing 1 missed cleavage for trypsin digestion. All instrument and acquisition parameters are now definitively established for the HEK293 samples analysis workflow.

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: File names contain clear fraction identifiers (01A-12A, 01B-12B). Methods states 'a total of 96 fractions which were consolidated into 24'. The 24 files represent 12 fractions run in duplicate (A and B sets).

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: File naming pattern shows A and B sets (e.g., 01A/01B, 02A/02B) representing duplicate runs of each fraction. This indicates 2 biological replicates of the fractionated sample.

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.evidence: Each fraction appears once per replicate (A or B set). No technical replicates within each biological replicate are evident from file names or methods.

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: 24 raw files total, representing 12 fractions × 2 biological replicates. Methods confirms 'consolidated into 24' fractions.

FactorValue[FractionIdentifier].evidence: Same as Comment[FractionIdentifier] - file names clearly indicate fraction numbers 01-12 for both A and B replicate sets.

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: File naming analysis reveals clear fractionation pattern: 24 files represent 12 basic pH reverse phase fractions run in duplicate (A/B biological replicates). Each fraction (01-12) has corresponding A and B files. No treatment conditions, compounds, or other experimental factors are evident from methods or filenames - this appears to be a straightforward fractionated proteome analysis of HEK293 cells without additional experimental variables. All file-level assignments are definitively established.
